message:
feat(cleanup): cleanup_preview_with_cx Cx-first variant (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Expand the Cx-first surface beyond the wezterm/sharding trail into
another subsystem. `cleanup.rs` previously had 0 `_with_cx` methods
despite 6 async fns driving sequential retention scans across
multiple tables.

Because `StorageHandle` still exposes no `_with_cx` variants (147
async fns without cx surface — see ft-xbnl0.2.x inventory), this
slice threads cx through the *seams* between storage queries
rather than inside them. Each table scan in `cleanup_preview_with_cx`
is preceded by `cx.checkpoint()?` which:

  * pre-cancelled cx: short-circuits before touching storage at all
    (first checkpoint at the top of the function).
  * late-cancelled cx (cancelled while a scan is running): next
    scan is skipped, function returns with an Error::Runtime
    carrying the cancellation reason and the stage at which it was
    caught.

Scan logic inside each `if config.retention_days > 0 { ... }` block
is otherwise identical to the legacy `cleanup_preview`.

Regression test (new)
`cleanup_preview_with_cx_matches_legacy_on_empty_db`:
  * Runs BOTH legacy and Cx-first variants against a fresh empty
    StorageHandle with a fresh Cx::for_request().
  * Asserts the two CleanupPlan results are bit-for-bit equivalent
    across all fields and every table summary.

Follow-ons: cleanup_apply_with_cx (same pattern, for delete path);
upgrading to StorageHandle._with_cx once storage.rs gains that
surface.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * cleanup:: — 95 passed / 0 failed (+1 new parity test; all
    existing legacy cleanup tests still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (1m00s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
